title: A dish my neighbor taught me
summary: Narrate the time a neighbor showed you how to make a dish you had never cooked before.
topic: A dish my neighbor taught me to cook
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences about a time a neighbor taught you to make a dish. Describe what the dish was, what happened during the cooking, and how it turned out. Use past simple.
hintStarters: My neighbor knocked on my door one afternoon and…
She told me to bring a big pot because…
I had never used that ingredient before, so…
When we finally tasted it, I thought…
sampleText: My neighbor Fatou knocked on my door on a rainy Tuesday and asked if I wanted to learn to make thiéboudienne, the Senegalese rice and fish dish. I said yes immediately. We went to her kitchen, which was much bigger than mine, and she put a large pot on the stove. She explained each ingredient slowly, which I appreciated because I did not know most of them. She showed me how to fry the fish first in a little oil with garlic. The smell was strong and wonderful. Then she added tomato paste, dried vegetables, and a whole piece of fermented shellfish paste that I was afraid of at first. Halfway through, I almost put in too much water and she quickly stopped me with a gentle laugh. She said her grandmother always told her, 'Less water, more patience.' We cooked it together for about an hour. When the rice was tender and had absorbed all the sauce, she turned off the heat and we sat down at her table. I ate two full plates. It was one of the best things I had eaten in months. On the way back to my flat, I wrote down everything I could remember so I could try it alone.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: giải thích từng nguyên liệu một cách chậm rãi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I was afraid of at first
stopped me with a gentle laugh
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ngăn tôi lại với một tiếng cười nhẹ nhàng
Ít nước hơn, nhiều kiên nhẫn hơn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: wrote down everything I could remember
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ghi lại mọi thứ tôi có thể nhớ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for a sequence of events
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể một câu chuyện có thứ tự, mỗi bước dùng past simple theo trình tự. Ví dụ: 'She added… then we cooked… when the rice was tender… she turned off the heat… we sat down.' Mỗi động từ là một bước trong chuỗi hành động.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When telling a story in order, each step uses past simple in sequence. For example: 'She added… then we cooked… when the rice was tender… she turned off the heat… we sat down.' Each verb is one step in the chain.

sampleTranslationVi: Người hàng xóm Fatou của tôi gõ cửa vào một buổi chiều thứ Ba mưa và hỏi tôi có muốn học làm thiéboudienne, món cơm cá của người Senegal không. Tôi đồng ý ngay lập tức. Chúng tôi vào bếp của cô ấy, rộng hơn bếp của tôi nhiều, và cô ấy đặt một chiếc nồi lớn lên bếp. Cô ấy giải thích từng nguyên liệu một cách chậm rãi, điều mà tôi trân trọng vì tôi không biết hầu hết trong số đó. Cô ấy chỉ cho tôi cách chiên cá trước trong một ít dầu với tỏi. Mùi thơm rất mạnh và tuyệt vời. Sau đó cô ấy thêm cà chua xay, rau khô, và một miếng mắm tôm lên men mà lúc đầu tôi hơi sợ. Đến giữa chừng, tôi suýt đổ quá nhiều nước thì cô ấy nhanh chóng ngăn tôi lại với một tiếng cười nhẹ nhàng. Cô ấy nói bà của cô ấy luôn nói: 'Ít nước hơn, nhiều kiên nhẫn hơn.' Chúng tôi nấu cùng nhau khoảng một tiếng đồng hồ. Khi gạo đã mềm và thấm hết nước sốt, cô ấy tắt bếp và chúng tôi ngồi xuống bàn. Tôi ăn hai đĩa đầy. Đó là một trong những thứ ngon nhất tôi đã ăn trong nhiều tháng. Trên đường về căn hộ, tôi ghi lại mọi thứ tôi có thể nhớ để có thể tự mình thử làm.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct way to say someone asked if you wanted to learn something.
options: She asked if I want to learn the dish.
She asked if I wanted to learn the dish.
She asked did I want to learn the dish.
She asked whether I want to learn the dish.
mcQuestions.explanation: In reported questions, the verb shifts to past: 'asked if I wanted' is correct. 'Ask did' is not standard reported speech.
mcQuestions.hint: What tense shift happens when you report a past question?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that uses 'show' correctly in the past.
options: She shown me how to fry the fish.
She shew me how to fry the fish.
She showed me how to fry the fish.
She shows me how to fry the fish.
mcQuestions.explanation: The past simple of 'show' is 'showed'. 'Shown' is the past participle used in perfect tenses.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the simple past tense of 'show'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'almost' before a near-mistake?
options: I almost put in too much water.
I was almost putting in too much water.
I almost did put in too much water.
I nearly almost put in too much water.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Almost + past simple' is the natural way to describe a near-mistake. Combining 'nearly' and 'almost' is redundant.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'nearly almost' correct? What is the simplest way to say you nearly did something?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct use of 'when' to link two past events.
options: When the rice was tender, she turned off the heat.
When the rice became tender, she was turning off the heat.
When the rice was tender, she is turning off the heat.
When the rice is tender, she turned off the heat.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'When + past simple, past simple' links two completed events in order. Both verbs in the past keep the story consistent.
mcQuestions.hint: In a sequence of past events, which tense should both verbs be?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the word that means liquid has been soaked up completely.
options: spilled
absorbed
poured
drained
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Absorbed' means soaked up. 'Drained' means removed liquid. 'Spilled' means accidentally overflowed. 'Poured' means moved liquid from one place to another.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means a material soaks up a liquid into itself?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the sentence that correctly expresses something you ate was very good.
options: It was one of the best things I have eaten in months.
It was one of the best things I had eaten in months.
It was one of the best things I ate in months.
It was one of best things I had eaten in months.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'One of the best things I had eaten' uses past perfect (had eaten) because the eating happened before the moment of speaking. The superlative 'the best' requires the definite article 'the'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense shows an action that happened before another past moment?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best sentence to describe writing something down in a hurry.
options: I wrote down everything I could remember.
I wrote down everything I can remember.
I write down everything I could remember.
I wrote down everything I remembered.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Could remember' shows ability in the past at that moment. 'Can' is present tense and breaks the past narrative.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past form of 'can'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase correctly means 'at the middle point of an activity'?
options: Halfway across
Halfway from
Halfway through
Halfway into
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Halfway through' is the fixed phrase meaning 'at the middle point of an activity or period'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition completes the fixed phrase 'halfway ___'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly uses 'asked' with a direct action.
options: She asked me if I would like come to her kitchen.
She asked me to come to her kitchen.
She asked me coming to her kitchen.
She asked that I come to her kitchen.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Ask someone to do something' (infinitive) is the standard pattern for a request.
mcQuestions.hint: What structure follows 'asked me' when making a request?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence where the adjective correctly describes a person who gives freely.
options: She was very generate with her time.
She was very generously with her time.
She was very generous with her time.
She was very generosity with her time.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Generous' is the adjective. 'Generously' is an adverb; 'generosity' is a noun; 'generate' is a verb.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'was', do you need an adjective, adverb, or noun?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the correct phrase for a meal eaten with another person.
options: We shared a meal together.
We shared a meal each other.
We shared a meal between us.
We shared a meal ourselves.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Share a meal together' is the natural collocation. 'Each other' needs a verb like 'gave each other'.
mcQuestions.hint: What word naturally completes 'share a meal ___'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Has a neighbor, classmate, or colleague ever taught you something practical that you still use? What made that kind of learning different from learning from a book?
